examples: With its earsplitting falsettos and crashing percussion instruments, Chinese opera is not for everyone.
Every day we jump out of bed with the earsplitting ring of the alarm-clock, swallow a quick breakfast and then rush to catch the bus.

The new paddles allowed players to hit with more force and the carbon made a louder, more earsplitting thwack.
In those times of turmoil and earsplitting acrimony in public life, the term referred to those Americans who didn't raise much commotion, but whose fidelity to tradition was unwavering.
